ratio: increases rapidly in the earlier stages of growth and increases less rapidly to maturity
axis_shape: varies from nearly straight to highly irregular, commonly curved in more than one plane
coil_tightness: inner volutions are more tightly coiled than outer ones
height_of_volution: increases with increasing radius
thickness_of_spirotheca: thin; wall thickness increases regularly through the smaller parts of the test and then less rapidly in the last stages of growth
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: irregular but tend to be tightly fluted across the entire length of the test; less fluted in the inner volutions; appear closely spaced
proloculus: spherical
tunnel_shape: poorly defined, wanders in the equatorial plane; tunnel height less than half of the chamber; tunnel width increases rapidly
chomata: weakly developed; low, discontinuous chomata or parachomata
axial_filling: present, especially in the inner volutions, but it is irregular

axis_shape: varies from nearly straight to highly irregular, commonly curved in more than one plane
coil_tightness: relatively tight with numerous septa in each volution
height_of_volution: increases with increasing radius
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed

chomata: well developed; low, discontinuous chomata or parachomata
axial_filling: present, especially in the inner volutions, but it is irregular

axial_filling: present, especially in the inner volutions, but it is irregular

shape: elongate cylindrical from the second whorl on
coil_tightness: inner volutions are more tightly coiled than outer ones
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: very thin in the median region, with high, narrow and numerous folds
proloculus: 180μ in diameter
tunnel_shape: regular with moderate width and height
chomata: narrow and weakly defined, in the first whorl only; in the succeeding whorls there are weak pseudochomata
axial_filling: very strong from the second to the penultimate whorl, in the form of a broad patch, which is widest in the third whorl

coil_tightness: inner volutions closely coiled and highly inflated fusiform to fusiform in shape; form ratios of all volutions essentially equal except for first or second volution
height_of_volution: slowly increasing toward outer; average height of fifth volution 0.2 mm
thickness_of_spirotheca: thin; average thickness of fifth volution above tunnel 0.037 mm
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: numerous, closely spaced, and intensely fluted throughout
proloculus: small, irregularly spherical in shape
tunnel_shape: narrow and moderately high, with somewhat regular path
chomata: well developed, high, asymmetrical, both tunnel and poleward slopes usually steep

coil_tightness: inner volutions are more tightly coiled than outer ones
thickness_of_spirotheca: varies in thickness throughout any given volution
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: intensely and irregularly fluted and are the most complex of any species of Fusulina yet observed from Texas; the intensity of fluting is as great in the central portions of the test as in the axial extremities
proloculus: minute
tunnel_shape: indistinct and cannot be accurately defined in any of the growth stages of the species
chomata: indistinct; pseudochomata made of fill around the septa

coil_tightness: inner volutions are more tightly coiled than outer ones
thickness_of_spirotheca: varies in thickness throughout any given volution
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed

chomata: indistinct; pseudochomata made of fill around the septa

ratio: near 1.5 in the first volution and increasing to near 2.5 at maturity
coil_tightness: whorls expand gradually and moderately rapidly
thickness_of_spirotheca: thin, reaching a maximum thickness of 15 to 20 microns in the outer volutions
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: strongly folded from pole to equator in all volutions
proloculus: moderately large
tunnel_shape: moderate width and height
tunnel_angles: varying somewhat irregularly within the limits of 20° and 30°, with no systematic change in successive whorls
chomata: narrow and slight, taking the form of a thickening of the septa adjacent to the tunnel rather than a levee-like ridge on the floor of the volution

equator: inflated central region; first volution inflated
coil_tightness: shell expands slowly and about uniformly
height_of_volution: less in the center of the shell, and they increase toward the polar ends
thickness_of_spirotheca: thickest in the regions of the tunnel; it becomes thin poleward
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: thin in the center of the shell and moderately widely spaced; highly fluted throughout the length of the shell, and brought in contact by the fluting for about half the heights of the chambers to the edges of the tunnel
proloculus: relatively large and spherical in shape; outside diameter of 190 to 260 microns
tunnel_shape: wide and its path is nearly straight
tunnel_angles: second through the fourth volution average about 27, 27, and 31 degrees, respectively
chomata: narrow and high with steep tunnel sides and steep poleward slopes; nearly three-fourths the heights of the chambers

coil_tightness: inner volutions are more tightly coiled than outer ones
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: thick, in the median and axial regions thickened by supplementary deposits, which also cover the base of the whorls; septal folds strong, numerous, high and narrow
proloculus: small, 26 and 145μ (the usual size)
tunnel_shape: regular with moderate width and height
chomata: present only in the first one or two whorls, in the succeeding whorls replaced by massive pseudochomata
axial_filling: in the axial regions thickened by supplementary deposits

shape: fusiform from the second whorl on
lateral_slopes: straight or slightly convex in the outer whorl
coil_tightness: spire rather high, expanding very slowly
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: in the outer whorls thinner than the wall, in the inner whorls comparatively thick and in the median region thickened by supplementary deposits; septal folds numerous, high and narrow
proloculus: large, from 182μ to 292μ in diameter
tunnel_shape: regular with moderate width and height
chomata: massive, subquadrate, present only in the first two whorls, after which they are replaced by pseudochomata and by supplementary deposits in the median region

coil_tightness: inner volutions are more tightly coiled than outer ones
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: rather closely spaced and regularly and strongly fluted with high, narrow folds; strong septal plication began at an early stage of growth
proloculus: prolocula range in diameter from about 120 to 240 microns
tunnel_shape: generally wider
tunnel_angles: averaging between 20° and 25°
chomata: deposit is laid down on the septa and roof as well as the floor, constricting or even filling the chambers for a short distance on each side of the tunnel, and even thickening the septa where they hang pendant above the tunnel

ratio: in third volution equal to 2.5-3.0, in the outer volutions varies from 2.9 to 4.17
coil_tightness: whorls coiling very uniformly
thickness_of_spirotheca: moderately thick; in the outer whorls it attains a thickness of 35-45μ, in the inner ones 25-35μ
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: fairly thick, somewhat thinner than the spirotheca, quite strongly folded; folds forming regular, not thickly set loops, some are fairly lofty, rounded and occasionally subsquare in shape, not traceable along the axis in the inner volutions because of the massive and compact axial filling
proloculus: 190-230, and often 190μ
tunnel_shape: regular with moderate width and height
chomata: Chomata absent
axial_filling: axial part considerably compact; axial filling well developed on 4-5 whorls

poles: a rounded end and an obtusely pointed end
coil_tightness: coiling is tight, gradually broadening
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: not constant in thickness, and are often quite thick with high, angular and irregular plications; the plications are irregularly distributed
proloculus: spherical, with a diameter of 99μ
tunnel_shape: regular with moderate width and height
chomata: small distinct chomata only in the first two whorls, and only pseudochomata in the following whorls

poles: uniform obtusely pointed ends in all the whorls
coil_tightness: inner volutions are more tightly coiled than outer ones
thickness_of_spirotheca: thin, usually not exceeding 0.030 mm in the outer whorls, seldom increasing up to 0.040 mm
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: intensely folded along the entire length of the whorls, forming high loop-shaped and triangular arches
proloculus: spherical, 0.14-0.16 mm in diameter
tunnel_shape: regular with moderate width and height
chomata: in the inner whorls are subquadrate, in the outer ones they become narrower, mushroom-shaped or in the form of curved, thin columns

shape: fusiform, flattened on one side and more inflated in the median region on the other side
equator: more inflated in the median region on the other side
poles: a sharply pointed pole and a blunted pointed pole
ratio: in the fourth whorl 1.7-1.8, in the sixth 2.0-2.6
coil_tightness: spire tightly coiled for two whorls, with gradual expansion in the succeeding ones
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: evenly but irregularly folded throughout the whorl, slightly twisted at the poles
proloculus: small, with an outer diameter of 29μ
tunnel_shape: regular with moderate width and height
chomata: present in the inner whorls, asymmetrical in form (sloping toward the poles and steep toward the aperture), partially subquadrate

shape: fusiform, flattened on one side and more inflated in the median region on the other side
equator: more inflated in the median region on the other side
ratio: in the fourth whorl 1.7-1.8, in the sixth 2.0-2.6
coil_tightness: spire tightly coiled for two whorls, with gradual expansion in the succeeding ones
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: evenly but irregularly folded throughout the whorl, slightly twisted at the poles
proloculus: small, with an outer diameter of 29μ
tunnel_shape: regular with moderate width and height
chomata: present in the inner whorls, asymmetrical in form (sloping toward the poles and steep toward the aperture), partially subquadrate

equator: in the initial one or two whorls shortened, with a convex median region
ratio: 2.8-3.5, more frequently 3.0
coil_tightness: spire loosely coiled, expanding uniformly
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed
septa: thin, of almost the same thickness as the wall; septal folding strong and irregular, with a predominance of high and very narrow, sometimes closed folds
proloculus: large, 205-345μ in diameter
tunnel_shape: regular with moderate width and height
chomata: absent
axial_filling: weak axial thickening in the early whorls

shape: subcylindrical with the flat central part; first volution fusiform, second to last subcylindrical
poles: rounded from the first to the fourth volution, but bluntly pointed in the outer volutions
ratio: 1.6 to 2.6; largest in the third and the fourth volution, then becoming smaller toward the last volution
coil_tightness: inner two to three volutions tightly coiled, the following four to six volutions loosely
height_of_volution: 33, 49, 66, 114, 148, 197 and 213 microns for first to seventh volutions; gradually increase outwards
endothyroid: Endothyroid coiling in the inner whorls not observed

proloculus: medium in size and spherical in shape; outside diameter 114 to 197 microns
tunnel_shape: gradually increases in width through growth, being indistinct at the last volution; path irregular; top fluted

axial_filling: dark-colored axial fillings commonly fill the chamber in the extreme polar regions of the first to the fourth volution
